bill.title: Veterans’ Compensation Cost-of-Living Adjustment Act of 2025

veteran.reason: The bill directly increases disability compensation rates for veterans with service-connected disabilities, making them explicit and immediate subjects.

has_disability.reason: The bill directly increases disability compensation rates for veterans with service-connected disabilities, making people with disabilities an explicit and immediate subject.

student.reason: This bill adjusts veterans' disability and survivor compensation rates and has no bearing on student status.

has_student_loans.reason: This bill adjusts veterans' disability and survivor compensation rates and has nothing to do with student loans.

parent_of_minor.reason: The bill increases additional compensation for dependents and dependency and indemnity compensation to children, so veteran or survivor households with minor children receive amounts that vary with having dependent children.

caregiver_of_adult.reason: The bill adjusts compensation rates for veterans and survivors and does not target caregivers of dependent adults.

homeowner.reason: Veterans' compensation COLA increase applies identically regardless of housing status.

renter.reason: The benefit adjustment does not vary by whether a recipient rents.

other.reason: Housing arrangement has no bearing on eligibility or effect of this compensation increase.

citizen.reason: Veterans' benefits eligibility does not turn on citizenship status; the COLA increase applies identically regardless of this trait.

permanent_resident.reason: Non-citizen veterans and survivors can qualify for these benefits; the bill's effect does not differ based on citizenship status.

other_status.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by immigration status; eligibility is based on veteran/survivor status, not citizenship.

single.reason: Disabled veterans receive base compensation regardless of marital status; being never-married does not create a distinct trait-dependent effect.

married.reason: The bill increases additional compensation for dependents under section 1115(1), which includes a spouse, so married veterans are directly and firsthand affected by the marital-status-dependent add-on.

divorced.reason: DIC and dependent compensation attach to current spouses and survivors, not divorced spouses, so divorced status is essentially irrelevant here.

widowed.reason: The bill directly increases dependency and indemnity compensation to surviving spouses under section 1311, making widowed survivors an explicit subject of the bill.

under_18.reason: The bill explicitly adjusts dependency and indemnity compensation payable to surviving children (sections 1313(a) and 1314), a benefit tied specifically to minor children, so this age group is a direct subject of part of the bill.

age_18_25.reason: Children's DIC can extend to student dependents up to age 23, so some in this bracket are directly affected by the rate increase, though most young adults are not addressed.

age_26_49.reason: Veterans and survivors of this age are affected, but the compensation increase applies identically regardless of age.

age_50_64.reason: The adjustment applies to veterans and survivors identically regardless of age.

age_65_plus.reason: Although many veterans are elderly, the rate increase applies identically across ages, so age itself does not change the effect.

under_30k.reason: VA disability and DIC compensation are not means-tested; the COLA increase applies identically regardless of household income.

income_30k_60k.reason: Benefit increase does not vary by income level.

income_60k_120k.reason: Benefit increase does not vary by income level.

income_120k_250k.reason: Benefit increase does not vary by income level.

income_250k_plus.reason: Benefit increase does not vary by income level.

education.reason: Not related to teaching or school staff.

healthcare.reason: Does not target clinical or allied health workers.

manufacturing.reason: Not relevant to industrial or production work.

agriculture.reason: No connection to farming or fishing.

transportation_logistics.reason: Not relevant to drivers or logistics work.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: The bill benefits veterans whose qualifying trait is prior military service, which falls under this bucket; however, the compensation depends on veteran/disability status rather than current occupation, so the tie is indirect.

professional_office.reason: Does not target white-collar office roles.

retail_service.reason: No connection to retail or service work.

other.reason: No distinct occupational group beyond veterans is targeted.

employee.reason: The bill adjusts veterans' compensation rates and applies regardless of employment status.

self_employed_owner.reason: Not relevant to self-employment; benefits flow to veterans irrespective of work relationship.

unemployed.reason: The compensation increase does not depend on whether a recipient is seeking work.

retired.reason: Benefits are tied to veteran/survivor status, not retirement; effect identical across statuses.

not_in_labor_force.reason: Though some disabled veterans may be unable to work, the bill's effect derives from veteran status, not labor-force position.

employer_plan.reason: Bill adjusts cash disability/survivor compensation and does not affect employer health plans.

marketplace_individual.reason: No connection to ACA marketplace or individual plans.

va_tricare.reason: The bill's subjects are disabled veterans and their survivors, who heavily rely on VA/TRICARE coverage, though the adjustment is cash compensation rather than a change to health coverage itself.

uninsured.reason: No effect on insurance status; the bill only changes compensation payment rates.

male.reason: The COLA adjustment applies to veterans and survivors regardless of gender; veteran male overrepresentation is not trait-dependence.

female.reason: Benefits apply identically regardless of gender.

nonbinary_other.reason: Benefits apply identically regardless of gender.

lgbtq.reason: This bill adjusts veterans' disability and survivor compensation rates and has no differential effect based on LGBTQ+ identity.

christian.reason: Veterans' COLA adjustment applies regardless of religion.

jewish.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

muslim.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

hindu.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

buddhist.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

other_faith.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

none.reason: No religious dimension to this benefits bill.

target_groups.reason: The bill directly increases disability compensation rates for veterans with service-connected disabilities.

other_criteria: surviving spouse of a deceased disabled veteran
target_groups.reason: The bill increases dependency and indemnity compensation paid to surviving spouses of certain disabled veterans.

other_criteria: surviving spouse of certain deceased disabled veteran
target_groups.reason: The bill increases dependency and indemnity compensation payable to surviving spouses of certain disabled veterans.

other_criteria: child/survivor of certain deceased disabled veteran
target_groups.reason: The bill increases dependency and indemnity compensation payable to surviving children of certain disabled veterans.
